build.notes: Produces vgi-example-worker/build/install/vgi-example-worker/bin/vgi-example-worker. Composite-builds vgi-rpc-java from ~/Development/vgi-rpc-java when present.

subprocess.notes: Windows: stdio subprocess via the native launcher wrapper (scripts/windows/vgi-java-launcher.c -> vgi-java-worker.exe; set VGI_JAVA_EXE/VGI_JAVA_CP). Spawns one JVM per pool worker. See docs/WINDOWS.md.

launch.notes: VGI launcher manages spawn + idle shutdown; the JVM stays warm across all bench ATTACHes so JIT actually gets to optimise the worker code.

launch-shm.notes: Same as launch but negotiates shared-memory transport (64 MiB segment). Massive win on Java — unix-socket framing was the bottleneck; SHM removes it and lets the JVM's off-heap ArrowBufs map directly. Throughput roughly doubles at t=4.

notes: vgi-java is the Java port of the VGI protocol (not vgi-rpc-java, which is the underlying RPC layer). Worker uses Java 21 toolchain (foojay-resolver auto-provisions if absent). Only the launch: transport is declared — subprocess would spawn a fresh JVM per query, which would dominate the measurement; the launcher keeps the JVM warm long enough for the JIT to compile hot paths.
